repo_purpose: Shinobi is an autonomous Windows-native agent designed for Spanish-speaking YouTubers, offering robust tools for content adaptation and automation.
architecture_summary: This repository is organized into various modules, each having specific functionalities: it includes the core project defined in `package.json`, the `src/watchers` for managing notifications with GitHub's API, and `scripts` for execution and testing operations. Documentation is extensively detailed in the `docs` folder, and the project also stores raw data in `artifacts`. Various reports and configurations are managed in the `misc` directory. While well-structured, the architecture lacks completeness regarding testing and error handling strategies, posing risks of operational failures. Moreover, the project is dependent on several external libraries, including `axios` and `playwright`, possibly affecting its stability in case of version updates.

modules.name: Core
modules.responsibility: Defines the main dependencies and configuration for the Shinobi application.

modules.name: Hermes Watcher
modules.responsibility: Handles release notifications from Hermes using GitHub API.

modules.name: Scripts
modules.responsibility: Contains various scripts for testing and generating different reports.

modules.name: Documentation
modules.responsibility: Provides comprehensive coverage of the Shinobi ecosystem, including roadmap and current project status.

modules.name: Artifacts
modules.responsibility: Stores raw data extracted from specific YouTube content.

modules.name: Miscellaneous
modules.responsibility: Includes various reports, configurations, and auditing files for managing project functionalities.

risks.description: No tests specified in package.json and lack of unit tests for major functions in critical modules.
Potential failures in network requests handling to GitHub API and error handling deficiencies.
Unreadable sections in some audit reports indicating hidden risks.
Several outdated dependencies and their associated maintenance concerns highlighted in audit reports.
Inconsistencies in roadmap documentation signal potential planning and strategic issues.
